name: Top 7 Pipedrive Alternatives for Growing Agencies
description:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

In an agency environment, evolution is everything. You need to recognize when it’s time to dig in and refine and when it’s time to, well, change course and move forward.

As your agency scales, juggling client pipelines, invoices, and campaigns in a limited-feature CRM like Pipedrive can feel restrictive. While Pipedrive is intuitive for small teams, growing agencies often need deeper performance visibility, built-in marketing tools, and better cross-departmental integration.

If you’re hitting those limits, it’s time to explore more capable options. In this guide, we’ve rounded up seven of the best Pipedrive alternatives built for agencies that want to streamline operations, manage revenue end-to-end, and deliver outstanding client experiences.

TL:DR: Top 7 Pipedrive Alternatives 

  • Pipedrive is simple to use but has certain limitations. Agencies often complain about constant add-ons, extra costs, and limited email/SMS automation.
  • The goal: Find a top Pipedrive alternative that manages sales, invoices, marketing, and client relationships from one dashboard.
  • Key criteria when switching:
    • Centralized client & sales workflows
    • Built-in invoicing and recurring billing
    • Multi-currency and tax-compliant payments
    • Automation for follow-ups and task reminders
    • Easy integrations with email, project, and payment tools

7 Best Pipeline Alternatives at a Glance 

  • Synup OS: Best for mid-sized marketing agencies. Manage leads, proposals, invoices, payments, and marketing from one dashboard.
  • HubSpot Sales Hub: Best for scalable growth. Pricing: free to US $150/user/month.
  • Zoho CRM: Best for budget-minded businesses needing automation and AI at low cost. Pricing: free for 3 users; from US $14/user/month.
  • Salesmate: Best for all-in-one sales, marketing, and AI support. Pricing: US $23–63/user/month.
  • Close CRM: Best for inside-sales teams focused on calls, SMS, and email outreach at scale. Pricing: US $9–139/user/month.
  • Salesforce Sales Cloud: Best for large or enterprise agencies. Pricing: US $25–350+/user/month depending on edition.
  • Monday CRM: Best for teams wanting visual, drag-and-drop pipelines with easy automations. Pricing: US $12–28/seat/month.

Why Switch from Pipedrive?

Source: Pipedrive

Pipedrive enjoys widespread appeal for its easy-to-use pipeline view and affordable entry-level pricing. But as agencies expand, a few pain points surface repeatedly:

  • Limited Marketing Automation: You’ll need third-party apps to handle campaigns, social media, or email workflows.
  • Manual Invoicing and Reporting: Financial tracking remains separate from CRM data.
  • Higher Costs for Advanced Features: The per-user pricing model adds up quickly as teams grow.
  • Lack of White-Label or Multi-Client Support: Pipedrive isn’t designed for agencies managing multiple client accounts simultaneously and doesn't offer deep customization.

If you want a single platform that supports everything from lead capture to invoicing, automation, and client reporting, you’ll find better fits among the tools below.

What Features to Look for in a Pipedrive Alternative

A Pipedrive alternative is only as good as how well it fits your agency’s size, workflow, and growth objectives. Larger agencies often prioritize integrations, automation, and client analytics. Smaller teams usually prefer simplicity, speed, and lower costs.

Start with these guiding questions and the specific features each scenario calls for:

  1. What Does Your Sales Process Look Like?

If your agency’s workflow stops at lead generation and deal tracking, a lightweight CRM with a visual pipeline might be all you need. 

But if you also handle invoices, proposals, contracts, and recurring retainers, choose a platform that goes beyond CRM and supports end-to-end client management..

Watch out for:

  • Drag-and-drop pipelines (e.g., Synup OS, Monday CRM, Salesmate) for simple deal tracking.
  • Smart invoicing and proposal tools (e.g., Synup OS) to send quotes, track opens, and automate billing.
  • Recurring payments and subscriptions for retainer clients.
  • Document tracking and e-signatures so your team knows when proposals are viewed or signed.
  • Automation workflows to move deals through stages or send reminders automatically.
  1. How Big Is Your Sales Team?

CRMs often charge per user, and that can add up fast. 

If you’re a small agency or freelancer team, pick one that offers free seats or flexible user tiers. Larger teams benefit more from role-based access and multi-pipeline management. Look for:

  • Free or bundled seats for small teams (Zoho CRM and HubSpot Free).
  • Unlimited pipelines and dashboards for growing teams (Salesmate, Monday CRM).
  • Role-based permissions to control access across departments.
  • Performance tracking dashboards to monitor conversions and team activity in one view.
  • Built-in communication tools like calling, chat, or internal notes (Close CRM, Salesmate).
  1. Which Integrations Are Must-Haves?

Your CRM should easily integrate with the tools you already use, whether they’re for marketing, invoicing, or customer support.

If you run campaigns for multiple clients, you’ll need integrations that connect sales with email marketing, social media, and analytics tools.

If you manage ongoing client work, look for connections in project management and invoicing. You'll need: 

  • Email marketing integrations (HubSpot Marketing, Synup OS).
  • Social media review and listings tools (Synup OS for agencies managing reputation and SEO).
  • Accounting or invoicing software integration like Stripe, Xero, QuickBooks, and PayPal.
  • Project management sync (Synup, Monday CRM).
  • Lead capture from forms and websites via Zapier or native forms.
  • Analytics and dashboard reporting to combine marketing and sales data for smarter forecasting.
  1. Do You Prefer Automation or Manual Control?

Smaller to medium-sized teams often prefer manual control to maintain that personal touch. But once your deal volume increases, automation becomes essential. So, look for:

  • Automated lead assignment based on territory or source.
  • Follow-up sequences for email and SMS.
  • Task reminders for meetings, proposals, or renewals.
  • AI-assisted lead scoring and forecasting (Synup, HubSpot, Zoho).
  • Smart notifications when clients open proposals or invoices (Synup OS).
  1. Are You Working Locally or Globally?

If your clients are international, your CRM needs to handle multiple currencies, taxes, and payment gateways.

Highlights to watch: 

  • Multi-currency invoicing (Synup OS, Zoho CRM, Salesforce).
  • Automated tax calculation by country or region.
  • Timezone-aware scheduling for meetings or follow-ups.
  • Global payment gateways like Stripe, PayPal, Payoneer, and Razorpay. 
Source

‍

Understanding these points helps you choose a platform that meets your urgent needs and can grow with your agency. With that in mind, let’s explore the top Pipedrive alternatives built for growing agencies and what they're really good at. 

‍

seo.title: 7 Best Alternatives to Pipedrive for Agencies
seo.description: Growing agencies need a scalable CRM. Check out 7 alternatives to Pipedrive that enhance automation and collaboration
